# Figure description: 51.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Геофизические_разрезы/51.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section / profile** (профиль), combining a **gravity anomaly curve** with a **geological-geophysical interpretation panel** at the top. It appears to be a Bouguer gravity anomaly profile with an overlying schematic geological cross-section, typical of Soviet-era regional geophysical survey documentation. The figure is labeled **ПРОФИЛЬ №** (Profile No.), likely Profile 1 or a numbered traverse line.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a regional geophysical traverse line across part of the survey area in northern Kazakhstan (map sheets M-42 and/or N-42, 1:200,000 scale). The profile extends horizontally across what appears to be a significant distance, with kilometer or point markers along the baseline. The top panel shows a schematic **geological cross-section** with multiple stratigraphic units and contacts annotated, while the lower portion shows the **gravity anomaly curve** plotted against a vertical scale. Several **borehole/drill point intersections** appear to be marked along the profile line (small circles on the curve), likely corresponding to reference wells (скважины). Specific borehole numbers are not clearly legible in this reproduction quality, but the format is consistent with скв-type annotations.

---

## Key Content

**Upper geological panel:**
- A schematic stratigraphic cross-section is shown across the top ~quarter of the figure
- Multiple lithological units are delineated by contacts, with density values annotated in the format **σ = X.XX г/см³** (grams per cubic centimeter), indicating rock density assignments used for gravity modeling
- Visible density values appear to include values in the ranges approximately **0.00, 2.00, 2.40, 2.60, 2.70 г/см³** and similar — these are likely the modeled densities of different geological formations (sedimentary cover, basement units, intrusive bodies)
- The cross-section shows several distinct blocks or layers, with vertical contacts suggesting faulting or block tectonics
- Hatching or boundary lines delineate different rock units

**Lower gravity profile:**
- A prominent **positive gravity anomaly** (Δg positive, upward deflection) is visible in the central portion of the profile, reaching a significant amplitude — possibly on the order of **+100 to +200 mGal** above the baseline based on the vertical scale markings
- To the right of the central high, there is a pronounced **negative gravity anomaly** (downward deflection), which appears to reach depths of approximately **−300 to −500 mGal equivalent** on the scale, suggesting a major density deficit — possibly a deep sedimentary basin, graben, or low-density intrusive body
- The left portion of the profile shows a gradual, broad **negative trend** before rising to the central high
- The extreme right portion of the profile shows **sharp, high-amplitude oscillations**, possibly indicating local structural complexity, faulting, or data artifacts near the profile end
- The overall pattern — broad negative flanks with a central positive — is consistent with a **basement horst or uplifted dense block** flanked by sedimentary basins

**Scale:**
- The vertical (Y) axis on the right side appears graduated in **mGal** (milligals), with values ranging from approximately **−1000 to +200 mGal** or similar, though the exact range is difficult to confirm at this image resolution
- Horizontal axis represents distance along the profile in **km** (kilometers), with markers at regular intervals

## Text and Labels

The following text is visible (transcribed as accurately as possible given image quality and orientation — note the image appears to be rotated/inverted in places):

- **ПРОФИЛЬ №** — "Profile No." (figure title at bottom, partially legible)

- Top of sheet (inverted): **Условные обозначения на листе №3** — "Legend/Conventional signs on sheet No. 3"
- Also inverted at top: **К отчету Западно-Сибирской партии за 1983–1984г.** — "To the report of the West Siberian Party for 1983–1984" *(note: this likely reads "Западно-Казахстанской" or similar, given the survey area)*
- Stamp/seal visible at lower left (partially legible): appears to read **№ 36572** — consistent with report number, plus институт/фонд archive markings
- **Чертёж №** / **Лист №** — "Drawing No." / "Sheet No." (lower margin)
- **сгр. 1 габус** or similar — possibly a depth/formation label at lower right

## Research Value

This gravity profile provides critical information about the **deep crustal and basement structure** along a key traverse in the survey area, revealing the geometry of density contrasts between basement highs, sedimentary basins, and potentially intrusive bodies. The large-amplitude negative anomaly on the right side of the profile may indicate a deep sedimentary graben or low-density pluton — both of which are significant targets for **mineral and hydrocarbon exploration**. Combined with the density-modeled geological cross-section in the upper panel, this figure allows a geologist to constrain the **depth, geometry, and lithological composition** of major subsurface structures, directly informing assessments of mineral potential (e.g., ore-controlling structures or basin thickness for hydrocarbons) in the northern Kazakhstan survey area.
